changes.before: Frostmourne did not cut the way other weapons cut. The sword drank.
"I know where the blade turns," she said. "I know the sequence. I don't know what the room will let me keep."
changes.after: "I know where the blade turns," she said. "I know the sequence. I don't know what survives the cast."

changes.before: The cold version found every old memory still lodged in her and iced it over.
changes.after: At the sound of that voice, every old memory still lodged in her went cold.

changes.before: Her feet obeyed a fraction late.
She rammed blue pressure through the stalled turn, a wedge where the blade expected flow, and the sword cracked like lake ice taking weight.
changes.after: She rammed blue pressure through the stalled turn, a wedge into the stalled channel, and the sword cracked like lake ice taking weight.

changes.before: The flame ran white-blue at the base where magic fed it, gold at the edges where the room's surviving heat answered.
changes.after: The flame ran white-blue at the base where magic fed it, gold at the edges where it caught what heat the room still held.

changes.before: Tears came by force before sorrow found room to arrive.
changes.after: Tears came by force before she had room for sorrow.

changes.before: He asked it like a commander because asking it any other way would have broken him.

Because neither of them had enough left to cut through thirty men and a ring of paladins while a damaged soul-drinking sword twitched at their feet. Because living long enough to speak later, as Uther had told her in the corridor, was the only work left that might still matter.
There was no first glance that turned kindly.
He took in the king, and whatever in him had remained held together only because action was still possible broke in one silent visible place across his face.
changes.after: He took in the king and went still.

changes.before: He asked it like a commander because asking it any other way would have broken him.
Because living long enough to speak later, as Uther had told her in the corridor, was the only work left that might still matter.
Whatever was happening in the throne room had already started.
changes.after: Arthas was already in the chamber with the king.

changes.before: look at the room, we killed him, you're standing in the answer.
changes.after: look at the room, we killed him.
